title: senecio eremophilus var. eremophilus
intro: Senecio eremophilus var. eremophilus, also known as Stinking Penny, is a plant species in the sunflower family native to Western Australia. It is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter tall, with small white flowers and a strong, unpleasant odor. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, Senecio eremophilus var. eremophilus is used to treat various health issues, such as colds, fever, and skin conditions, and is often made into a tea or tincture to promote digestion and relieve respiratory problems.
